data.title: AI TRENDS | AI Accelerates Crypto Attacks, Says Ledger CTO
data.description: Ledger CTO Charles Guillemet has highlighted the increasing role of artificial intelligence in facilitating crypto attacks, making them faster and more cost-effective. According to NS3.AI, over $1.4 billion in assets were stolen or lost in crypto attacks over the past year, as reported by DefiLlama. Guillemet noted that AI enhances the speed of vulnerability discovery and exploit chaining. He emphasized that formal verification and hardware-based security provide stronger protection for wallets and protocols.

data.title: Tesla's Q1 2026 Deliveries Miss Wall Street Expectations, Stock Drops 5.4%
data.description: Tesla reported first-quarter 2026 vehicle deliveries totaling 358,023 units, falling short of Wall Street's consensus estimate of approximately 365,645 vehicles. According to NS3.AI, this shortfall led to a 5.4% decline in Tesla's stock on Thursday. In response, Goldman Sachs adjusted its price target for Tesla from $405 to $375, while Truist also lowered its target. Both firms maintained their Hold ratings on the stock.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Anticipates Agreement with Iran by Monday
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed optimism about reaching an agreement with Iran by Monday. According to Foresight News, Trump stated that negotiations are currently underway with Iran. He mentioned that if an agreement is not reached promptly, he is considering taking drastic measures, including seizing control of oil resources. Trump believes that an agreement could be finalized as early as tomorrow.

data.title: Rumors of Trump's Hospitalization Debunked by White House
data.description:
On April 5, local time April 4, a rumor about U.S. President Donald Trump being hospitalized due to illness quickly spread across the U.S. internet. According to BlockBeats, White House Communications Director Steven Cheung refuted the claims, stating that President Trump was actively working at the White House and the Oval Office throughout the Easter weekend. Following a post on Trump's social media account about a rescued missing pilot, Cheung reiterated that journalists questioning the president's whereabouts should take note of his ongoing activities.

The White House also addressed the online speculation through its official account, criticizing those who concoct conspiracy theories when President Trump does not engage with the media for 12 hours, while noting the lack of similar scrutiny when Biden does not interact with the media for extended periods. The White House assured the public that President Trump has not ceased his duties and condemned the accounts spreading the hospitalization rumors as "complete idiots."

On April 4, President Trump's schedule was abruptly limited to "executive time," and the White House declared a media "lid," indicating no further public appearances for the day. Social media users interpreted the "lid," Trump's absence from public view, and the subsequent lack of public activities as potential evidence of him being treated at Walter Reed Hospital.

data.title: Bitcoin's Five-Year Performance Compared to Other Assets
data.description:
Peter Schiff recently commented on Bitcoin's performance over the past five years, noting that its price has increased by only 12%. According to Odaily, during the same period, the Nasdaq index rose by 57.4%, the S&P 500 index by 59.4%, gold by 163%, and silver by 181%. Schiff questioned the rationale for holding Bitcoin if its appeal lies in superior long-term performance.

Michael Saylor responded by emphasizing the importance of the time frame. He stated that since August 2020, Bitcoin has been the best-performing mainstream asset, with a clear advantage. Saylor suggested that extending the time span would only widen this gap.

data.title: An address deposited 10 million STO tokens into Bitget, and its peak unrealized profit reached $52.33 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, an address received 30.99 million STO tokens an hour ago, equivalent to approximately $5.98 million, and then deposited 10 million tokens, worth $1.92 million, into Bitget 45 minutes ago. Tracing the source of the funds, these tokens were accumulated by two addresses between October 2025 and January 2026 at an average price of $0.1713. This sale will yield a profit of $207,000. These 30.99 million tokens previously had a floating profit of $52.33 million when the price peaked at $1.86 on April 2nd.

data.title: Saylor responded to Peter Schiff: Bitcoin has been the best-performing mainstream asset since August 2020.
data.description: Peter Schiff wrote that Bitcoin's price has only risen 12% over the past five years. In contrast, the Nasdaq rose 57.4%, the S&P 500 rose 59.4%, gold rose 163%, and silver rose 181% during the same period. If Bitcoin's appeal lies in its superior long-term performance, then why should anyone continue to hold it? Michael Saylor responded, "The time frame is crucial. Since August 2020, Bitcoin has been the best-performing mainstream asset, and the advantage is very clear. If you extend the timeframe, this gap only widens."

data.title: Fox News: If Iran doesn't reach an agreement soon, Trump is considering blowing everything up and taking over the oil.
data.description: According to Fox News: US President Trump stated that if a deal with Iran is not reached soon, he is considering blowing everything up and taking over the oil. Trump believes an agreement could be reached with Iran as early as tomorrow. (Jinshi)

data.title: The White House denies rumors that Trump is seriously ill and hospitalized.
data.description: On April 4th local time, news of Trump's serious illness and hospitalization quickly went viral on American social media. In response, White House Communications Director Steven Cheung posted a denial: President Trump has been working tirelessly in the White House and the Oval Office this Easter weekend. After Trump's social media account posted the news that the "missing pilot had been rescued," he posted again: "Let those disgusting journalists who question the president's whereabouts see what President Trump is doing." The White House also responded to online speculation through its official account: "When President Trump goes 12 hours without speaking to the media, those mentally unstable liberals start making up ridiculous conspiracy theories. (While they say nothing when Biden often goes 12 days without speaking to the media.) Don't worry! President Trump has never actually stopped working." Furthermore, the White House angrily denounced the account that posted the "Trump seriously ill and hospitalized" rumor as "a weapons-grade moron." It is understood that on April 4th local time, Trump's schedule suddenly only listed "executive time," and that morning the White House announced the media term "lid," implying that the president would not make any public appearances that day. Social media users interpreted the word "lid," Trump's absence from public appearances, and subsequent lack of public activity as potential evidence that he was admitted to Walter Reed Hospital for treatment.

data.title: Analysis: Trump's remarks indicate that there has been no progress in US-Iran negotiations.
data.description: According to Al Jazeera analysts, it's clear from Trump's latest post that there has been no progress in negotiations, or any effort to bridge the differences between Iran and the United States. Given what we've heard from the Iranian Revolutionary Guard in the past few hours—that any attack on civilian infrastructure will be met with equal retaliation—we are at a very critical juncture. Trump has threatened to destroy Iranian bridges and power plants, while Iran has threatened to retaliate in kind, targeting civilian infrastructure in the Gulf region and Israel. (Jin Shi)

data.title: OPEC+ Expresses Concerns Over Energy Asset Attacks Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description:
OPEC+ has raised concerns about the impact of attacks on energy assets during the ongoing conflict involving the United States and Iran. According to BlockBeats, the committee emphasized the importance of securing international maritime routes to ensure the continuous flow of energy. The statement highlighted the high costs and lengthy time required to restore damaged energy infrastructure to full operational capacity, which could affect overall supply capabilities.

PolyBeats monitoring indicates that on the prediction market Polymarket, the probability of WTI crude oil closing above $130 per barrel in April has risen to 51%, while the likelihood of it exceeding $120 has increased to 80%.

data.title: Bitcoin Wallets Experience Significant Losses Amid Bearish Sentiment
data.description: Bitcoin wallets holding between 100 to 10,000 BTC are experiencing substantial losses, according to Glassnode data. These wallets are realizing over $200 million in daily losses based on a 7-day moving average. According to NS3.AI, the bearish sentiment surrounding Bitcoin has intensified, with Santiment reporting that bearish discussions are at their highest since late February. The ratio of bullish to bearish comments stands at 0.81, indicating a predominance of negative sentiment in the market.

data.title: Robert Kiyosaki Highlights Impact of 1974 Policy Changes on Money and Retirement Systems
data.description: Robert Kiyosaki has emphasized the significant impact of policy changes in 1974 on the money and retirement systems. According to NS3.AI, Kiyosaki argues that these shifts are currently exerting pressure on savers. He continues to advocate for Bitcoin, gold, and silver, which he refers to as 'real money.'

data.title: Michael Saylor Shares Bitcoin Tracker Update
data.description:
On April 5, Michael Saylor, founder of Strategy, released new information regarding the Bitcoin Tracker. According to BlockBeats, Saylor commented, "₿ack to Work."

Historically, Strategy has disclosed additional Bitcoin acquisitions the day after such announcements.

As of the last disclosure, Strategy holds a total of 762,099 Bitcoins, with a total cost of approximately $57.69 billion, averaging a holding cost of about $75,694 per Bitcoin.

data.title: Michael Saylor has released another Bitcoin Tracker update; he may disclose his accumulating data next week.
data.description: Michael Saylor, founder of Bitcoin treasury company Strategy, once again posted information related to Bitcoin Tracker with the caption "₿ack to Work". Based on past patterns, Strategy always discloses information about increasing its Bitcoin holdings the day after such news is released.

data.title: Trump threatens to strike Iranian power plants and bridges
data.description: US President Trump posted on social media that Tuesday will be both "Power Plant Day" and "Bridge Day," which will happen simultaneously in Iran.

data.title: Spark Completes First Month of Buyback Program
data.description: Spark has successfully completed its first month of buyback transactions, spending 571,957 USDS to purchase 26.66 million SPK tokens. According to Foresight News, the average price per SPK was $0.0215. This initial buyback represents approximately 1% of SPK's circulating supply. The buyback initiative is planned to continue for 12 months, with an estimated total buyback of 12% of the circulating supply, equating to around 320 million SPK.

data.title: Trump Announces Planned Strikes on Iranian Infrastructure
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced on Truth Social plans to target Iranian power plants and bridges on Tuesday. According to BlockBeats, Trump described the day as 'Iran's power plant and bridge day, combined into one,' calling it an unprecedented move. He urged the opening of the strait, warning of severe consequences if not complied with.

data.title: Trump: F-15 pilot successfully rescued; press conference to be held Monday
data.description: US President Trump tweeted: "We have successfully rescued a seriously wounded and extremely brave F-15 crew member/officer from deep in the Iranian mountains. The Iranian military was conducting a massive search and had closed in. He is a highly respected colonel. Such raids are extremely rare due to the high risk to personnel and equipment, and almost never happen! The second operation followed the first, when we rescued the pilot in broad daylight, which was equally unusual, and we remained over Iranian airspace for seven hours. All those involved demonstrated incredible courage and capability! I will hold a press conference with the military in the Oval Office at 1 p.m. (local time) Monday (1 a.m. Beijing time the following day)."

data.title: Iran launches its 96th wave of strikes against the US and Israel
data.description: The Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) issued a statement on May 5th, stating that its navy and aerospace forces launched the 96th wave of Operation True Commitment-4 in the early hours of the morning. The statement indicated that the IRGC launched a fierce attack on the Haifa oil refinery, which supplies fuel to Israeli warplanes, resulting in the destruction of its core components; it also used missiles to attack the natural gas facilities of ExxonMobil and Chevron in Habshan, UAE, and a petrochemical plant in Ruwais, UAE. Furthermore, multiple petrochemical facilities in Sitra, Bahrain, and Shuaiba, Kuwait, were also attacked by drones and missiles, causing complete shutdowns and large-scale fires. (CCTV News)
